Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Both groups have similar activities ApplicationReceived BasicScreening SkillTest PersonalityAssessment CulturalFitCheck FinalInterview HiringDecision The columns include things like LocalResident ProfessionalAssociation SkillScore PersonalityScore CulturalFit ScoreAdjustment Decision and Resource

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Group A Protected Group all entries have LocalResident as FALSE and ProfessionalAssociation as None Their scores are recorded without any adjustments

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now Group B Unprotected Group has some entries where LocalResident is TRUE and ProfessionalAssociation is LocalBusinessLeadersClub For U001 they have a CulturalFitCheck with a plus 5 adjustment because of the association Their CulturalFit score is adjusted from 60 to 65 Similarly U003 also gets a plus 5 boost adjusting their CulturalFit from 58 to 63 However U002 who does not have the association does not get any adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the key difference here is that in Group B candidates affiliated with the LocalBusinessLeadersClub receive an extra 5 points in their CulturalFit score This adjustment is not present in Group A

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Bias in hiring can occur when certain groups receive preferential treatment not based on merit Here the plus 5 adjustment for being part of a specific club could be seen as favoring those candidates

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Even if their actual CulturalFit score is lower they get a boost which might give them an advantage over others who are not part of the club

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For example U001 had a CulturalFit of 60 which was adjusted to 65 Similarly U003 score went from 58 to 63

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In Group A there is no such adjustment All scores are as they are and decisions are based purely on the scores without any boosts

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This could disadvantage candidates not part of that club even if they have similar or better inherent qualifications

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: If the hiring process includes adjustments based on affiliations that are not directly related to job performance it can lead to unfair advantages

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This might perpetuate inequalities if access to such clubs is limited to certain demographics For example if the LocalBusinessLeadersClub is more accessible to a particular group then giving them a boost could systematically favor that group leading to discrimination against others

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It is important for hiring processes to be based on objective criteria related to the job requirements rather than external affiliations

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: If the club membership is considered a valid indicator of cultural fit then maybe the adjustment is intentional However without clear criteria linking the club membership to job performance this adjustment could be seen as arbitrary and biased

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also looking at the outcomes In Group B both U001 and U003 who got the boost were hired while U002 without the boost was not

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: The presence of the adjustment in Group B might have influenced the hiring decisions especially if the adjusted scores pushed candidates over a threshold For instance if the cutoff for hiring is a certain CulturalFit score the plus 5 could make the difference between being hired or not even if the underlying score is lower

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the bias is present in Group B process due to the score adjustment based on professional association membership

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This undermines fairness by introducing a factor unrelated to the candidates actual skills or fit potentially leading to inequitable outcomes
